content.rendered:
Events

The Marshall-New Era Neighborhood Association encourages our neighbors to get out and meet each other. Throughout the year we host various events. We also post events from other organizations on our Blog page. Go to our Subscribe Page and add your email address to get updates on the events below.

  • Monthly Board Meetings: MNENA meets on the third Monday of each month (except December) at the Society Church building, 2711 B St. Meetings start at 6:00 pm and everyone is welcome. We have a small, finger-food type potluck available for those who would like to participate. Representatives from the City Police and other governmental agencies often stop in to give us updates. If you’d like something to be put on the agenda, write to info@marshallnewera.org
  • Neighborhood Cleanups: Every quarter we organize a cleanup somewhere in the neighborhood. Notices are sent out to everyone on our email list with the locations. We start at 8:30 am with coffee and bagels, then supply all the things you’ll need (gloves, pickers, trash bags, and friends). Watch for announcements on this website or through our email blasts.
  • National Night Out: Each year we join in with the National Night Out events that occur all around the nation. NNO enhances the relationship between neighbors and law enforcement while bringing back a true sense of community. In Midtown, we partner with other neighborhood associations for a progressive dinner. The next National Night out is held on the 1st Tuesday in August.
  • Holiday Party: We have a great party in December. It’s usually a potluck with a very small amount of “business”, mostly announcing our accomplishments and talking about what we want to do the next year.
  • Special Events: Throughout the year we have special events, sometimes in partnership with the Boulevard Park Neighborhood Association. Watch this website and sign up for email updates. We hope you can join us!

Spring Block Party & Plant Swap

Join your neighborhood associations for our Spring Block Party! Family friendly activities for all ages. Music, food, and games. Meet your neighbors and make new friends. Learn about what’s going on in our community, get involved, and celebrate the reopening of the New Era Community Garden.

There will be a “Plant Swap” for those who want to bring a plant and swap it for another. Bring your seeds, starts, or plants you have too many of and swap them for something else.

Admission is FREE!

Hosted By: Marshall-New Era & Boulevard Park Neighborhood Associations

Who: All residents are welcome! You do not need to be a member of the neighborhood associations to attend.

Date: Saturday, April 18, 2026

Time: 11am – 4pm (including Food Truck);

Location: 26th St and C St cul-de-sac

Volunteer: We need volunteers! Click here if you can help out with the event, the link has several areas where we need help.

Donations: We are collecting donations for local non-profits supporting our community. Please consider donating travel-sized toiletries, socks, undergarments, and gently-used shoes.

Transportation – We encourage neighbors to walk or bike to the party. Limited street parking is available near Leland Stanford Park, one block from the event.

Flyer – Download the event flyer here.

Our Community Garden has been reborn! After 2 years sitting vacant the Marshall-New Era Neighborhood Association was able to secure a 5 year lease on the property and re-start the local garden at 26th and B Sts. in the fall of 2024. The 41 plots were all snapped up quickly, but we keep a waiting list for those who are interested.

We’ve also started a “Friend of the Garden” program, where folks can enjoy spending time in the garden without managing a garden plot, or while they’re on the garden wait list to get a plot! The annual FOG membership is $20, and in addition to having access to the garden you’ll be invited to all garden events including our potlucks.

The major highlights of 2025 for the garden are:

  • First and foremost, clearing out all the weeds and overgrown brush to prepare the garden plots for spring planting;
  • Building a beautiful new garden shed;
  • Winning an Integrated Pest Management (IPM) grant from Sacramento city, which will provide funds to purchase native and drought tolerant plants to attract garden pollinators;
  • Hosting a spring block party at the community garden with the Marshall-New Era Neighborhood Association, which was well attended and enjoyed by hundreds of folks; and
  • Organizing potluck dinners and brunches at the garden, so gardeners could connect in a more social setting (ie without getting their hands dirty!).

If you would like more information about the garden, would like to be added to the waitlist for a plot, or want to become a Friend of the Garden, please reach out by emailing garden@marshallnewera.org

Join Us

MNENA members work to make this a better neighborhood by 

  • Advocating on your behalf with the City on Homelessness and other issues
  • Keeping you updated by the Police Department on Crime and Prevention
  • Meeting regularly with your Councilmember and Assemblymember or their staff
  • Tracking building permits so we can inform you of major projects
  • Quarterly Cleanup Events 
  • Notifying you via email and blog posts of community activities
  • and more

We’d love to have you join us as a voting member of MNENA. Membership is annual (January -December). Those who join after September 1st will have their membership extended through the following year. Dues are $ 20 for an individual/household, $ 35 for a business. Click this link if you are a renewing member for the payment option. New members should click for a membership application and follow the instructions.

If you’re not ready to join us yet, click here to get 2 or 3 emails per month on what is happening in the neighborhood.

If you have any questions, contact info@marshallnewera.org
